weather.tip: Overcast at 20.8°C with a moderate 39% rain chance today — no umbrella emergency, but keep one within reach and build a little buffer into your commute in case a shower hits during peak hours.

around.headline: Woman on a test drive hits a pedestrian in Bengaluru after pressing the accelerator instead of the clutch
around.summary: A pedestrian was injured after a customer on a car test drive mixed up the accelerator and clutch pedals. If you're accompanying a test drive as a dealership staffer or bystander nearby, keep well clear of the vehicle's path until the driver is confirmed comfortable with the controls.

around.headline: Bengaluru cricketer blackmailed with an intimate video after a WhatsApp call at a hotel
around.summary: A budding cricketer was blackmailed after a WhatsApp video call turned into a recorded honey-trap, with the blackmailer demanding money to avoid the footage being leaked. This is a classic sextortion setup — see today's street-smart scenario for exactly how to respond if it happens to you.

around.headline: Chemical leak after a truck overturns on the Tumakuru–Bengaluru highway
around.summary: A truck carrying chemicals overturned on the Tumakuru–Bengaluru highway, disrupting traffic in the area. If you're travelling that stretch today, check live traffic updates before you set out and expect diversions near the spill site.

around.headline: Rs 5,000 fine proposed for driving vehicles on Bengaluru footpaths
around.summary: The state's Development Minister has pushed for raising the fine for riding or driving on footpaths to Rs 5,000, aiming to keep walking space clear of encroaching two-wheelers and cars. If enacted, expect stricter footpath enforcement in the coming weeks, especially in congested commercial stretches.

around.headline: Bengaluru–Mysuru highway toll hiked again, just three months after the last increase
around.summary: Toll rates on the Bengaluru–Mysuru highway have gone up again, only three months after the previous hike. Frequent travellers on this route should factor the higher toll into weekend trip budgets or top up FASTag balances accordingly.

around.headline: US strikes Iran for a 12th consecutive night; Houthis claim attack on two Saudi oil tankers
around.summary: The US-Iran conflict has stretched into a 12th night of strikes, and Houthi rebels separately claim to have hit two Saudi oil tankers, raising fresh risk to Gulf shipping routes. Keep an eye on petrol and diesel prices over the next few weeks, since prolonged Gulf tension typically feeds through to pump prices.

events.name: Anubandha — Hindustani Classical Music Festival
events.note: A guru-disciple themed Hindustani classical music festival happening today — a good pick if you're into Indian classical music.

events.name: Special Onam trains via Bengaluru (Mysuru–Kannur)
events.note: Special trains have been added between Mysuru and Kannur via Bengaluru for the Onam rush — useful if you're planning festival travel to Kerala.

events.name: Global Chess League 2026
events.note: Bengaluru continues to feature prominently as teams battle it out in this year's league — worth catching if you follow competitive chess.

events.name: 1500 Years of Indian Visual Storytelling — exhibition
events.note: An ongoing exhibition tracing 1,500 years of Indian visual art and storytelling — a solid gallery outing this week.

events.name: Bengaluru Poetry Festival — 10th edition
events.note: The city's flagship poetry festival returns for its 10th year — worth watching for the schedule if you're into spoken word or literature.

street_smart.title: Someone you've been chatting with online video-calls you late at night, the call turns intimate, and minutes after you hang up they send screenshots from the call demanding money within the hour or they'll send it to your family and social media contacts. What do you do?
street_smart.based_on: Real incident today: a budding cricketer in Bengaluru was blackmailed with an intimate video recorded during a WhatsApp call, with the blackmailer demanding money to avoid it being leaked (The Times of India).
street_smart.situation: The message arrives with a countdown-style threat — pay a specific UPI ID within the hour, or the recording gets sent to your contacts and posted online. You don't recognise the number, and the profile you'd been talking to appears to have been created recently.

choices.text: Stop replying, don't send any money, screenshot every message and the profile/number as evidence, block the account, and immediately file a complaint via the National Cyber Crime Helpline (1930) or cybercrime.gov.in.
choices.feedback: Correct. Sextortion scams rely on panic and speed — reporting fast lets police and the helpline request takedowns from platforms before wider sharing happens, and your evidence (screenshots, UPI ID, number) is what makes the complaint actionable. Paying is never required to trigger a takedown.

choices.text: Pay the amount they're demanding to make it go away quickly.
choices.feedback: This is the trap. Paying doesn't delete anything — it confirms you're willing to pay, and scammers almost always come back demanding more, sometimes repeatedly over weeks. There is no version of this where one payment ends it.

choices.text: Just block the number and the account right away, without reporting it anywhere.
choices.feedback: Blocking stops the immediate messages but throws away your best window to act — once blocked, you may lose access to the chat needed for evidence, and without a police/helpline complaint there's no official request to stop the content from being shared or to catch the person doing this to others too.

choices.text: Reply and try to negotiate a lower amount, or plead with them to delete it.
choices.feedback: Engaging at all signals you're rattled and willing to deal, which keeps you in their loop and can drag out the pressure for longer. It wastes the early window that's best spent preserving evidence and filing a report instead.

takeaways: Never pay a sextortion demand — it doesn't guarantee deletion and almost always leads to repeated, escalating demands.
Preserve evidence first: screenshot the chat, the profile, the UPI ID or number, before blocking.
Report immediately via the National Cyber Crime Helpline (1930) or cybercrime.gov.in — these complaints are handled confidentially, and platforms can be asked to take down content quickly once filed.

quiz.q: What fine has Bengaluru's Development Minister proposed for driving or riding vehicles on footpaths?
quiz.why: A separate Rs 25,000 fine already exists for dumping waste on footpaths, part of the same push to keep walking space usable.

quiz.q: How many months after the previous increase was the Bengaluru–Mysuru highway toll hiked again, per today's report?
quiz.why: Frequent hikes on this stretch have made it one of the more closely watched toll routes for regular weekend travellers.

quiz.q: As of today's reports, how many consecutive nights has the US now carried out strikes on Iran?
quiz.why: The conflict has run in parallel with Houthi attacks on Gulf shipping, adding pressure on global oil prices.

quiz.q: Which Indian state has the longest coastline?
quiz.why: Gujarat's coastline runs to roughly 1,600 km, the longest of any Indian state.

quiz.q: What does GST stand for in India's tax system?
quiz.why: GST replaced a patchwork of central and state indirect taxes when it was rolled out nationwide in July 2017.
